label: Neuro-symbolic models

node.label: Neuro-symbolic models
node.definition: A neuro-symbolic model combines learned continuous representations with symbolic rules, programs, constraints, knowledge graphs, or proof procedures.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Neuro-symbolic models is modeled as depending on Neuro-symbolic & physics-informed learning, Constraint propagation, logic & program execution and feeding downstream nodes such as Theorem proving & symbolic deduction, Automated theorem proving & mathematical AI.
examples: atlas downstream link to Theorem proving & symbolic deduction
atlas downstream link to Automated theorem proving & mathematical AI
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Neuro-symbolic & physics-informed learning, Constraint propagation, logic & program execution.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Neuro-symbolic models has a `trained_under` dependency on Neuro-symbolic & physics-informed learning with `core` strength.
Neuro-symbolic models is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Constraint propagation, logic & program execution.
In this atlas graph, Neuro-symbolic models has a `represents_with` dependency on Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas with `common` strength.
In this atlas graph, Neuro-symbolic models has a `specializes` dependency on Transformers with `common` strength.
